author: judge-correctness-0715

dimensionScores.rationale: prior scored 4.28; I score 4.4 because c4's two outstanding opt-in EDINET P2s are now fixed in source (edinet.py:60 drops interim contexts, :130-140 prefers consolidated) and the FSDS segmented-row disaggregation is dropped at edgar_fsds.py:73 (test_fsds.py:72 asserts $350B consolidated, not the $56.8B segment) — no P0/P1, sole residual F6 is a low-impact opt-in completeness gap explicitly better than base.

prior 4.15; I score 4.25 because 227 pytest + 62 vitest pass green (runs/pytest.txt, vitest.txt) and the three fixes now carry fixtures the c4 EDINET P2s lacked (tests/test_edinet.py:72/94/101, test_fsds.py:54); held below higher by real gaps F2 (compute_ratios reflection loop untested, ratios.py:123 blanket except) and F3 (run_loop # pragma: no cover, service.py:422).

prior 4.03; I score 4.0 (flat) because nothing in the security surface changed this round — findings.json has zero security-dimension findings, EDINET/free-key providers stay OFF by default (edinet.py:197 gates on key) with trusted hardcoded API_BASE, no new exposure.

prior 3.68; I score 4.0 because the exact hotspot c4 capped on — the 617-LOC ingest/enrichment.py — is now a 19-line re-export facade (enrichment.py:11-19) over enrich/{us,eu,uk,jp,_base}; residuals are smaller bounded P2 opportunities (return-rule triplication F1 ranks.py:36, build_symbol_snapshot 5-concern snapshot.py:42, UI type-only cycle F5).

prior 4.25; I score 4.25 (flat) — no perf-relevant change this round; the sole finding F7 (reconcile.py:72-94 nested per-cell .loc over periods x ~25 columns) is a pre-existing bounded low-impact slow path, not a regression, behind opt-in deep-history.

topFindings: All three targeted fixes are real and test-guarded with no P0/P1 remaining: FSDS segmented-row skip (edgar_fsds.py:73, test_fsds.py:72 asserts consolidated $350B), EDINET consolidated-preference+interim-drop+docType-120 (edinet.py:60/130-140, jp.py:58, 3 new tests), and the 617-LOC enrichment.py split into a 19-line facade.

Objective gates are ground truth and green: 227 pytest + 62 vitest pass, ruff clean, construct verify --strict exit 0; VERIFY.json 14/14 findings supported, 3/3 honeypots caught, 0 refuted/unsupported — grounding is clean, no hallucinated citations.

Residual is 7 P2 opportunities, zero defects: the only correctness one (F6 EDINET amended-annual docType-130) is low-impact on an opt-in free-key provider and strictly better than base; the rest are maintainability/test/perf polish (F1 triplication, F2 untested compute_ratios, F7 reconcile nested .loc).

author: judge-pipeline-0715

dimensionScores.rationale: Pipeline intact end-to-end: the region split (src/crible/ingest/enrich/{eu,us,uk,jp,_base}.py) preserved all 9 functions + 6 constants + heartbeat calls behind a 19-line facade (enrichment.py); 227 pytest green, construct-verify strict exit 0. EDINET annual-filter fix (enrich/jp.py:58, ANNUAL_DOC_TYPES) is a real correctness improvement over the base that mis-booked interim as annual. Only correctness finding is F6 (P2, low): the {120} filter also drops amended annual 130s — collateral of a correct fix on an opt-in OFF-by-default path. No P0/P1. Prior 4.28; I score 4.3 — behavior-preserving refactor plus a correctness-improving EDINET fix, no offsetting regression.

227 pytest + 62 vitest green, ruff clean, construct-verify strict FR coverage; each enrichment function has a dedicated FR test (test_fr010_esef, test_fr016_edgar, test_fsds, test_edinet, test_companies_house) and run_refresh is tested (tests/test_refresh.py). Real gaps per lens: F3 — run_loop (service.py:422, the docker ingest --loop deployment entrypoint) is # pragma: no cover, no test references it; F2 — compute_ratios (widest column producer) has no direct test (silent column loss); F1 — return-6m rule coded 3x with no parity golden. Prior 4.15; I score 4.1 — the split added no new daemon-path tests and run_loop stays uncovered (persistent, not a regression).

Self-hosting NFRs (zero keys, zero CDN) hold on the default path: EDINET is free-key opt-in and returns skipped without a key (enrich/jp.py:27-29); ESEF/EDGAR keyless; EDGAR sends a proper SEC User-Agent (recent b803a05b). Blanket except-Exception guards are defensive resilience (outages recorded, resume), not swallowed auth. Import-only refactor adds no attack surface; the annual filter narrows ingested docs. Prior 4.03; I score 4.0 — nothing security-relevant changed.

prior scored 3.68; I score 3.9 because the 617-LOC enrichment hotspot was cleanly split by region (eu 213 / us 248 / uk 69 / jp 80 / _base 40 LOC) behind a 19-line facade (src/crible/ingest/enrichment.py) with every function/constant/heartbeat preserved and 227 tests green — a concrete, test-backed readability win. Held below 4 by remaining debt: F3 (run_loop's six copy-pasted time-gated blocks duplicate run_refresh's enrichment sequence, service.py:422 vs :281) and F4 (build_symbol_snapshot 5-concern 70-line hotspot).

Refactor is import-only, no runtime change; EDGAR bulk (one companyfacts.zip -> all issuers, enrich/us.py:154 iter_bulk_companyfacts) and FSDS depth use memory-safe iterators. Sole perf finding is F7 (P2 low): reconcile.py:72 nested per-cell .loc on deep-history symbols — bounded slow path. Prior 4.25; I score 4.2 — no perf regression, one persistent bounded hotspot.

topFindings: F3: run_loop (service.py:422), the docker ingest --loop self-hosted deployment entrypoint, is # pragma: no cover with no test referencing it, and hand-rolls six time-gated blocks that duplicate run_refresh's (tested) enrichment sequence — a real coverage + DRY gap on the primary deployment path.

The 617-LOC enrichment hotspot was split by region into small files behind a 19-line facade with all 9 functions, 6 constants and heartbeat calls preserved and re-exported — verified lossless against the pre-split commit; 227 pytest + 62 vitest green, ruff clean, construct-verify strict exit 0.

Self-hosting NFRs hold: EDINET is free-key opt-in and OFF by default (enrich/jp.py:27), ESEF/EDGAR keyless, so the default path stays zero-key; the only correctness gap (F6, P2) — annual filter excludes amended 130s — sits on that OFF-by-default path, minimal blast radius.

author: judge-reliability-0715

dimensionScores.rationale: The 4 recent changes are correct and regression-free under adversarial check. FSDS whole-entity retention holds: src/crible/providers/edgar_fsds.py:73 drops only dimensional rows (empty/absent segments stay), locked by tests/test_fsds.py:54 (segmented row listed first) while the base non-segmented test at :39 passes. EDINET consolidated/rank precedence (src/crible/providers/edinet.py:130-138) is a min over lexicographic key (is_nonconsol,rank) — order-independent across every pair ordering; the interim guard (edinet.py:60) now also rejects quarter-end balance instants. Two residual opt-in EDINET P2s the c4 anchor flagged are resolved with tests. Only residual is F6 (findings.json): amended-annual docType 130 excluded — opt-in, off by default, strictly better than base, a completeness gap not a defect. Prior scored 4.28; I score 4.3 because two prior EDINET P2s were fixed and a real-data FSDS segment mis-booking guard was added.

227 pytest + 62 vitest green (runs/pytest.txt, runs/vitest.txt), ruff clean. The order-dependent blind spot is explicitly defended: tests/test_fsds.py:54 and tests/test_edinet.py:72 both list the wrong-answer row FIRST so the guard fails-closed if removed; test_edinet.py adds interim-balance rejection and docType-120-only cases. Residual test gaps are opportunities, not live: F2 (compute_ratios reflection swallows exceptions, ratios.py:123, no direct test) and F3 (run_loop pragma:no-cover, service.py:422). Consistent with c4's 4.15.

No security-relevant change this cycle. EDINET is free-key opt-in and OFF by default (edinet.py:197, verified by test_edinet_provider_is_off_without_a_key), FSDS/EDINET URLs are hardcoded-trusted (SEC / api.edinet-fsa.go.jp — no SSRF), and the c4 mirror byte-ceiling (mirror.py) is unchanged. The 4 changes add no new exposure. Matches prior 4.03.

The 617-LOC enrichment.py hotspot that anchored maintainability for three cycles is now a 19-line facade (src/crible/ingest/enrichment.py) delegating to enrich/{eu,jp,uk,us}.py, each <250 LOC, with imports preserved and the suite green. Prior scored 3.68; I score 3.8 because that single largest structural item is resolved. Next-tier concentration is now honestly surfaced as P2 opportunities: run_loop (service.py:422, 106 LOC, pragma:no-cover) and build_symbol_snapshot (snapshot.py:42, 5 concerns).

Unchanged and strong. FSDS streams num.txt row-by-row (edgar_fsds.py:161, no OOM on the 1GB-VPS target); the new segments/precedence checks are O(1) per row/fact — no hot-path cost. Incremental compute stays dirty-only. F7 (reconcile per-cell .loc double loop, reconcile.py:72) is a newly-articulated but bounded low-impact slow path over a few periods x ~25 columns. Same as prior 4.25.

topFindings: The 4 recent changes are correct and test-invisible-regression-free: FSDS whole-entity retention (edgar_fsds.py:73) and EDINET's order-independent consolidated/interim precedence (edinet.py:130-138) are each locked by a first-writer-wins-defeating test; base cases still pass.

Two residual opt-in EDINET P2s from the c4 anchor are now resolved (consolidated-preference tiebreak + interim balance-instant guard), and the long-standing 617-LOC enrichment.py hotspot is dissolved into a facade + region files.

No live P0/P1; 227+62 green, ruff clean, construct verify --strict exit 0. Residual is 7 P2 opportunities (F6 amended-annual 130 excluded is opt-in/low; F3/F4 maintainability; F7 bounded perf) — none block the 80 bar.
